 {"id": "franken_networkx-2u4", "title": "Extract flow algorithm epsilon values to named constants", "description": "## What

Replace all inline 1e-10 values in fnx-algorithms min_cost_flow with the named constant
FLOW_EPSILON (already defined as 1.0e-10 at the top of lib.rs).

## Status: COMPLETED (March 2026)

All 5 occurrences in min_cost_flow have been replaced:
- Line ~19755: supply/demand balance check
- Line ~19765: supply node finder threshold
- Line ~19766: demand node finder threshold  
- Line ~19784: Bellman-Ford residual capacity and distance relaxation
- Line ~19822: flow output threshold (only emit non-zero flows)

## Why FLOW_EPSILON specifically

The min_cost_flow algorithm uses successive shortest paths with Bellman-Ford relaxation.
The 1e-10 threshold serves as a numerical stability guard:
- Supply/demand: total_supply must equal total_demand within FLOW_EPSILON
- Residual: only traverse edges with residual > FLOW_EPSILON (avoids floating-point ghosts)
- Output: only report flows above FLOW_EPSILON

NetworkX uses exact integer arithmetic for its network simplex, so our float-based
successive shortest paths needs this epsilon to avoid infinite loops from rounding.

## Files Changed

- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 25, ~19755, ~19765, ~19766, ~19784, ~19822)

## Verification

- cargo test -p fnx-algorithms passes (595 tests)
- Python test suite passes (1082+ tests)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:03:31.541611556Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:01.146759523Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:01.146045561Z", "close_reason": "All 5 inline 1e-10 values replaced with FLOW_EPSILON constant. Verified: 595 Rust tests + 1082 Python tests pass.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-2uf1", "title": "Convert: reject multigraph edge keys for Graph/DiGraph", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-11T13:51:56.396131038Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-11T13:53:46.849663017Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-11T13:53:46.849404905Z", "close_reason": "Reject non-multigraph edge keys in conversions; warn+drop in hardened", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-2zl", "title": "Small Utilities: eulerize, moral_graph, equivalence_classes, misc", "description": "## Scope
Implement ~15 small utility functions:
- eulerize(G): add minimum edges to make graph Eulerian. Find odd-degree nodes, compute minimum weight matching, add matching edges. O(V^2) for matching.
- moral_graph(G): moralize a DAG — add edges between co-parents (nodes sharing a child), then make undirected. Used in Bayesian networks.
- equivalence_classes(iterable, relation): partition elements by equivalence relation. Union-find based, O(n * alpha(n)).
- chordless_cycles(G): find all chordless (induced) cycles. Each cycle has no chord connecting non-adjacent vertices.
- minimum_cycle_basis(G, weight=None): find minimum weight set of cycles that form a basis for cycle space. Uses Horton's algorithm, O(V * E^2).
- find_asteroidal_triple(G): find an AT triple (three nodes where between each pair there exists a path avoiding neighbors of the third). O(V^3).
- restricted_view(G, filter_node, filter_edge): create a filtered view of G with only nodes/edges passing filters.
- subgraph_view(G, filter_node, filter_edge): alias for restricted_view with different API.
- reverse(G) / reverse_view(G): reverse edges of a DiGraph. reverse creates a copy, reverse_view creates a view.
- to_directed(G) / to_undirected(G): global conversion functions.
- number_of_edges(G) / number_of_nodes(G) / nodes(G) / edges(G) / degree(G) / neighbors(G): global accessor functions wrapping graph methods.

## Test Requirements
- eulerize: verify result is Eulerian (all even degree, connected)
- moral_graph: verify co-parents are connected, verify undirected
- equivalence_classes: verify partition property (complete, disjoint)
- chordless_cycles: verify no cycle has a chord
- minimum_cycle_basis: verify independence, verify spanning cycle space
- NX cross-validation on all functions", "status": "closed", "priority": 4, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-22T02:19:47.942297731Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.144660756Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.144136194Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 13 small utilities: eulerize, moral_graph, equivalence_classes, minimum_cycle_basis, chordless_cycles, to_directed, to_undirected, reverse, nodes, edges, degree, number_of_nodes, number_of_edges.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms", "tier-4"]}
@@ -131,7 +132,7 @@
 {"id": "franken_networkx-a59", "title": "Triads & Census: triadic_census, all_triads, triad classification", "description": "## Scope
Implement 6 triad analysis functions:
- `triadic_census` — count all 16 triad types in a directed graph (Batagelj-Mrvar O(V*E) algorithm)
- `all_triads` — generate all triads in a directed graph
- `is_triad` — check if a graph is a valid triad
- `triad_type` — classify a triad into one of the 16 types (MAN classification: 003, 012, 102, 021D, 021U, 021C, 111D, 111U, 030T, 030C, 201, 120D, 120U, 120C, 210, 300)
- `triads_by_type` — group triads by their type
- `within_inter_cluster` — count within-cluster and inter-cluster triads

## Algorithmic Approach
- triadic_census: Implement Batagelj-Mrvar algorithm. Enumerate ordered triples, classify by mutual/asymmetric/null dyads. O(V * avg_degree^2).
- all_triads: Enumerate all 3-node subsets, yield as subgraph. O(V^3) but can prune disconnected.
- triad_type: Count mutual, asymmetric, null dyads between the 3 pairs. Map to MAN notation.

## Dependencies
- Requires DiGraph support (already implemented)

## Implementation Location
- Rust: `crates/fnx-algorithms/src/triads.rs` — for triadic_census performance
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/triads.py`

## Test Requirements
- Verify census sums to C(V,3) for all graphs
- Test on known graphs with known census values
- Cross-validate against NetworkX", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:12:47.325910056Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T03:20:48.630769687Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T03:20:48.630240084Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 5 triad functions: triadic_census (MAN classification of all 16 types), all_triads, triad_type, is_triad, triads_by_type. Total: 475 functions.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms", "tier-3", "triads"]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-ahcp", "title": "ACCEPTED-DEBT: Drawing/layout NX delegations — inherently require matplotlib (15 functions)", "description": "## Functions that will REMAIN as NX delegations (15)
These are drawing and layout functions that inherently require matplotlib/numpy and cannot meaningfully be implemented in Rust:

Layout functions (in drawing/layout.py):
1. spring_layout → nx.spring_layout (requires scipy force simulation)
2. circular_layout → nx.circular_layout
3. random_layout → nx.random_layout
4. shell_layout → nx.shell_layout
5. spectral_layout → nx.spectral_layout (requires numpy eigendecomposition)
6. kamada_kawai_layout → nx.kamada_kawai_layout (requires scipy optimization)
7. planar_layout → nx.planar_layout
8. rescale_layout_dict → nx.rescale_layout_dict

Drawing functions (in drawing/nx_pylab.py):
9-15. draw, draw_spring, draw_circular, etc. → all require matplotlib

I/O format functions:
16. to_latex / write_latex → requires TeX formatting
17. write_graph6 / write_sparse6 / write_pajek / write_gexf → NX serializers

## Rationale
These are NOT technical debt — they are external-library integrations where the value of a Rust rewrite is zero. The graph data is small relative to the rendering/IO cost.

## Action
Document these as accepted NX delegations. No further work needed.", "status": "closed", "priority": 4, "issue_type": "docs", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:56.958983733Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-06T00:09:27.454903920Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-06T00:09:27.454643216Z", "close_reason": "Documented: ~15 drawing/layout functions (spring_layout, circular_layout, etc.) and ~10 exotic math functions (communicability_exp, spectral_graph_forge, etc.) remain as NX delegations. These are justified: drawing inherently requires matplotlib, and exotic math functions require numpy eigendecomposition/matrix exponential. Total justified NX delegations: ~25 functions.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-ak4", "title": "Connectivity & Disjoint Paths: edge/node disjoint paths, dominating sets", "description": "## Scope
Implement 8 connectivity and disjoint path functions:
- `edge_disjoint_paths` — find maximum set of edge-disjoint paths between s and t
- `node_disjoint_paths` — find maximum set of node-disjoint paths between s and t
- `all_node_cuts` — enumerate all minimum node cuts
- `connected_dominating_set` — find a connected dominating set
- `is_connected_dominating_set` — verify if a set is a connected dominating set
- `is_kl_connected` — test if graph is (k,l)-connected
- `kl_connected_subgraph` — find (k,l)-connected subgraph
- `connected_double_edge_swap` — perform double edge swap maintaining connectivity

## Algorithmic Approach
- edge_disjoint_paths: Decompose max-flow into edge-disjoint paths. Run max-flow with unit capacities, then extract paths by DFS on flow graph.
- node_disjoint_paths: Node-splitting technique (split each node into in/out with unit capacity edge), then max-flow + path decomposition.
- all_node_cuts: Use Kanevsky's algorithm. Enumerate all minimum vertex cuts.
- connected_dominating_set: Greedy approximation. Start with spanning tree, prune leaves.
- is_kl_connected / kl_connected_subgraph: Check/find subgraph where removing any k nodes leaves graph l-connected.
- connected_double_edge_swap: Randomly swap edges while maintaining connectivity check.

## Dependencies
- **Depends on Flow Algorithms** (bead 1) for max-flow based path decomposition
- Depends on existing connectivity functions

## Test Requirements
- Verify disjoint paths are actually disjoint
- Verify path count matches Menger's theorem (equals min-cut)
- Verify dominating set properties (every non-member has a neighbor in set)
- Cross-validate against NetworkX oracle", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:12:01.693008504Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T18:45:39.243270020Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T18:45:39.242760546Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 8 connectivity functions: edge_disjoint_paths, node_disjoint_paths (max-flow decomposition), all_node_cuts, connected_dominating_set (greedy), is_connected_dominating_set, is_kl_connected, kl_connected_subgraph, connected_double_edge_swap. Total: 561 functions.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms", "connectivity", "tier-2"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "franken_networkx-ak4", "depends_on_id": "franken_networkx-hp3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:15:28.420967793Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

   g8-raptorq:
     name: G8 RaptorQ scrub

@@ -33,3 +33,38 @@ jobs:
         run: maturin develop --features pyo3/abi3-py310
       - name: Run Hypothesis property suite
         run: pytest tests/python/test_hypothesis.py -v --tb=long
+
+  parser-fuzz-soak:
+    name: Nightly Parser Fuzz (${{ matrix.target }})
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 90
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        include:
+          - target: fuzz_edgelist
+            corpus: fuzz_edgelist
+          - target: fuzz_json
+            corpus: fuzz_json
+          - target: fuzz_graphml
+            corpus: fuzz_graphml
+          - target: fuzz_gml
+            corpus: fuzz_gml
+          - target: fuzz_adjlist
+            corpus: fuzz_adjlist
+          - target: fuzz_node_link
+            corpus: fuzz_node_link
+          - target: fuzz_attribute_value
+            corpus: fuzz_attribute_value
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+        with:
+          components: llvm-tools-preview
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          workspaces: fuzz
+      - name: Install cargo-fuzz
+        run: cargo install cargo-fuzz
+      - name: Run nightly parser fuzz soak
+        run: cd fuzz && cargo +nightly fuzz run ${{ matrix.target }} corpus/${{ matrix.corpus }} -- -max_total_time=3600

 [[package]]

@@ -143,7 +143,7 @@ This project uses four pervasive disciplines:
 - **Rust core:** 12 workspace crates including `fnx-cgse` (CGSE engine). `fnx-algorithms` is 40,181 LOC covering 280+ algorithms across shortest path, connectivity, centrality, clustering, matching, flow, trees, Euler, DAG, traversal, community, isomorphism, and more.
 - **Python bindings:** 12,154 LOC in `crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs` with 551 GIL-releasing `py.allow_threads(...)` call sites and zero callbacks into upstream NetworkX.
 - **Python package:** 446 public exports — 41 Rust-native, 306 Python wrappers, 89 NX-delegated (see [`docs/coverage.md`](docs/coverage.md) for the machine-checked breakdown). The NX-delegated functions are exotic algorithms (graph edit distance) and formats (Pajek/LEDA) outside V1 scope.
-- **Test suite:** 1,394 Python parity tests pass against upstream NetworkX (54 skipped) in ~20s. 8 Rust CGSE unit tests. 5 parser fuzz targets. 1 nightly Hypothesis property suite.
+- **Test suite:** 1,394 Python parity tests pass against upstream NetworkX (54 skipped) in ~20s. 8 Rust CGSE unit tests. 7 parser fuzz targets with committed seed corpora, PR smoke coverage, and nightly soak coverage. 1 nightly Hypothesis property suite.
 - **NetworkX backend mode:** entry points registered; ~80 functions dispatch to Rust via `backend.py:_SUPPORTED_ALGORITHMS`.
 - **CI:** G1-G8 fail-closed gate topology in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (fmt → clippy → rust tests → python tests → e2e → docs → conformance → performance → UBS → fuzz smoke → RaptorQ scrub).
 - **CGSE (Canonical Graph Semantics Engine):** `fnx-cgse` crate landed with 12-variant `TieBreakPolicy` sum type, `ComplexityWitness` with length-prefixed Merkle decision-path hash, `WitnessSink`, `WitnessLedger`, and V1 policy registry mapping 12 reference algorithms to their canonical policies.

+graph [
+  directed 0
+  node [ id 0 label "a" ]
+  node [ id 1 label "b" ]
+  edge [ source 0 target 1 label "alpha & beta" ]
+]

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<graphml xmlns="http://graphml.graphdrawing.org/xmlns">
+  <key id="d0" for="edge" attr.name="enabled" attr.type="boolean"/>
+  <graph id="G" edgedefault="undirected">
+    <node id="a"/>
+    <node id="b"/>
+    <edge source="a" target="b">
+      <data key="d0">true</data>
+    </edge>
+  </graph>
